title: Why Do People Look Plastic After Botox & Dermal Fillers？ | Dr Tim's Tips
filename: Why Do People Look Plastic After Botox & Dermal Fillers？ ｜ Dr Tim_s Tips.en.vtt
excerpt: Here's an interesting one why do people look plastic after Botox and dermal fillers well the short answer to this is because they've had too much what's been done in the wrong way the long answer is more interesting our brains are highly tuned to pick up what natural is and you c…

segments.text: here's an interesting one why do people look plastic after Botox and dermal fillers well the short answer to this is because they've had too much what's been done in the wrong way the long answer is more interesting our brains are highly tuned to pick up what natural is and you cannot full a human brain and very easily so we can pick up subtleties that aren't quite right about the movement and all that even the way the light reflects off skin that isn't usual and up that's because that's what our brains are effectively doing what real beauty is the sign the signs and signals that a healthy person gives off and that's natural beautiful health so anything that deviates from that our brain is sensitive to picking up from because we want to pick up if there's something wrong with the people around us and because we're trying to invest our time and effort and ultimately this is the instinct by the way it's not your principles and we're trying to invest the time and effort in people who will support your survival that's the evolutionary explanation so a small

difference in what is or a small deviation away from what is natural our brains will often pick it up and so what people are picking up is often it's unnatural movement or a lack of movement so if you actually picture and if you actually speak to people your eyebrows are moving all the time and this is for example is giving people that sense that you're processing what you're saying and what they're saying and it's part of a natural look I've noticed myself to analyzing faces that sometimes if there's zero movement it takes me a little bit longer to build up trust with people who don't have a lot of lower face expression so lower fat if you're a big smiley person and you're smiling a lot and you talk you're extrovert and you talk a lot and you can probably build trust perfectly well but if you happen to be more upper face mobile and you suddenly freeze all of that and some people will I think takes a bit longer to build trust there's a loss of warmth in some people and you need to look out for this I will say to people that I know well if I feel this is happening and that they might may be losing something and certainly over the years

many of my patients will have slightly less treatment as a result and then they're happier but they understand why they're not getting rid of every wrinkle depends on the patient's values as well I wouldn't necessary impose that on them I want them ultimately to feel confident that's the ultimate goal so sometimes you might put up with a degree of looking slightly treated in order to improve someone's confidence it's always a balancing act and other things that make people look plastic is the proportions are wrong so if you imagine trying to sculpt a face i've tried sculpting recently and it's really difficult making it look natural it's high knee contour change tiny shift in proportion and you can tell this is not a human face it's someone having a go and that process of making a human face while also altering it with a substance that isn't that wasn't innately there it's very easy to create something that doesn't look quite natural and our brains as i already said i extremely attuned to spotting what is natural and what's not and this is why tiny shifts in the position of the cheek or the volume or the projection of the

chin our brains can spot that's not natural and this does not fit in with what a natural human face looks like and our brains pick it up so what we need to do to prevent this is to take into account firstly the dynamic part of beauty so faces should move naturally and the volume and the position of delft alone where you put it affects this and of course the effects of botulinum toxins also affects this really is the ultimate skill of medical esthetics where you need to be honing your artistic skills so one of the things i am now currently doing a my career is sculpting and it's a brilliant way of making yourself more conscious of what a natural contour will be but you also need to be become an observer of human faces what is natural beauty look like and in a dynamic fit way you can learn a lot of this from watching television actually because that's a one way medium where you get to watch them without looking strange or staring at people on the bus or wherever and so but that's a great way of understanding normal natural human faces and then observe the difference so taking a short

video of your patients before and after can be another way that you can see the difference in movement and but all of these become ultimately your ultimate skill and as an aesthetic clinician is the artistry that supports the anatomy and the safety and the psychology and all the other stuff that we need to know and but that would be my main Tara's become an artist
